titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Fitz-Greene Halleck Collection
titlestmt.author: Mary A. Caldera

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 29, 2025 at 23:09

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of letters from and to Fitz-Greene Halleck, autograph manuscripts of several poems, a photograph portrait, and a day book. Correspondence, all autograph letters, signed, includes letters to John Bell Bouton and Frederic S. Cozzens and from Henry Wadsworth Longfellow and Samuel Ward. The "Day Book N. 10, R. Elliot" of Guilford, Connecticut, in the hand of Halleck, documents accounts for the period from June 1809 to May 1810.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired from various sources. The 2025 Addition, purchased from Bulls Head Rare Books on the D. Newton Barney Fund and the Danford N. Barney, Jr. Fund, 2025.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call numbers: Za Halleck, Uncat Za File 18, Uncat Za File 19, Uncat Za File 20, Uncat Za File 616.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1829-1867. II. Writings, 1809-18[64?]. III. Other Papers, 1809-1810. IV. 2025 Addition.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Fitz-Greene Halleck Collection. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

did.unittitle: "The Culprit Fay," poem, autograph manuscript, bound with illustrations
"On the Death of lieut. William Howard..."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The addition consists of correspondence, a portrait of Halleck, and a manuscript copy of "Young America."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Removed from: Drake, Joseph Rodman. The Culprit Fay and Other Poems.

did.unittitle: Kemble, Frances Anne "Fanny", regarding Halleck memorial
Legal document related to Jacob Barker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of a carte de viste and a printed etching of Halleck in the 1860s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound manuscript in Halleck's hand. Later inscription, "Presented to the Metropolitan Society Fair by Robert Bonner".
